public_functions.behavior_description: Initialize the eGTM-based 3-phase inverter PWM. Steps: 1) Enable the eGTM module and enable required clocks for the ATOM time base and DTM dead-time generation (FXCLK_0 for ATOM and CLK0 for DTM). 2) Initialize a unified PWM configuration with defaults, select ATOM submodule in the chosen cluster, and set center-aligned mode with a 20 kHz base frequency. 3) For three channels (base + 2 synchronous), set initial phase = 0, independent duty initialization, and attach DTM dead-time of 1.0 microsecond for both rising and falling edges on each channel to produce complementary high-/low-side outputs. 4) Assign output pin mappings for each channel’s primary and complementary outputs using the generic pin-map macros (driver handles muxing and pad settings). 5) Configure a period-event interrupt on the base channel: route it to CPU0 with the specified priority, and leave interrupt pointers for the remaining sync channels as NULL. 6) Install the ISR handler in the CPU interrupt system. 7) Initialize the PWM driver with the populated config and channels, update internal AGC/TGC references, and start all configured channels synchronously to guarantee a common time base (syncStart). 8) Enable synchronous update behavior (handled through configuration and unified driver) so that duty updates across channels maintain coherent timing. 9) Configure the LED GPIO as a push-pull output and drive it to a known initial state. The ISR will toggle this LED at each PWM period event. Error handling: This function assumes that all driver API calls succeed. If any driver API returns a status or error code (e.g., clock enable, PWM init, ISR install), production code should check these results, take appropriate action on failure (e.g., disable PWM outputs, abort initialization), and log or propagate the error status via application-level mechanisms since the function is void.

Perform a ramp update of the three PWM channel duties with wrap-around. For each of the three channels: if current duty + STEP reaches or exceeds 100%, reset that channel's duty to 0% before applying the increment. Then add STEP to each channel’s duty. After updating the three duty values in the local duty array, apply all new duties immediately using the unified driver’s multi-channel immediate update API so all channels update coherently in the same PWM cycle (syncUpdate honored). Error handling: This function assumes the driver update call succeeds. If the API returns a status, production code should check and handle a failure (e.g., retain previous duty values and set an application error flag) and propagate status via application-level mechanisms.

private_functions.behavior_description: PWM period ISR for the base ATOM channel. Responsibilities: acknowledge/clear the PWM period event interrupt flag for the configured ATOM channel/timer to prevent retrigger; toggle the status LED on port P13.0 using IfxPort to indicate activity; keep the ISR short (no blocking or heavy computation) to minimize latency and jitter; if shared data is updated, use atomic operations or set a flag for the main loop; follow the driver-required sequence (e.g., read-then-clear or write-1-to-clear) when clearing the interrupt source.
